summary: This joint resolution (Public Law 118-1) expresses Congress's disapproval of the action taken by the District of Columbia Council in approving the Revised Criminal Code Act of 2022 (D.C. Act 24-789). The District of Columbia Council enacted the Revised Criminal Code Act on January 17, 2023, and transmitted it to Congress on January 27, 2023, pursuant to section 602(c)(1) of the District of Columbia Home Rule Act. Under the Home Rule Act, Congress possesses the authority to review and overturn legislation passed by the D.C. Council. By enacting this joint resolution, Congress exercises that authority to formally reject the Revised Criminal Code Act of 2022, thereby blocking the local law from going into effect. The resolution was considered and passed by the House of Representatives on February 9, 2023, and by the Senate on March 8, 2023. It was subsequently approved and became law on March 20, 2023.
